description:
## Component: hx-icon-button (NEW)
**Tier 1 — Universal Foundation | Industry Consensus: 5/6 libraries**

Icon-only button with mandatory accessible label. Used for toolbar actions, close buttons, and compact UI patterns in healthcare dashboards.

### ADR-001 Classification: Property-Driven (Atomic)
Atomic component. Properties control behavior and accessibility. Default slot accepts the icon element.

### API Design

**Properties:**
- `label`: string (REQUIRED — accessible name, renders as aria-label)
- `variant`: primary | secondary | tertiary | danger | ghost
- `hx-size`: sm | md | lg
- `type`: button | submit | reset
- `disabled`: boolean
- `href`: string (renders as `<a>`)
- `name` / `value`: for form submission

**Slots:**
- Default slot: Icon element (hx-icon, svg, or img)

**CSS Parts:**
- `button`: The native button/anchor element
- `icon`: Icon container

**Events:**
- `hx-click`: Fires on click (composed: true)

### Drupal Integration
```twig
{# Close button on dialog/alert #}
<hx-icon-button label="Close" variant="ghost">
  <hx-icon name="x-mark"></hx-icon>
</hx-icon-button>

{# Edit action in data table row #}
<hx-icon-button label="Edit patient {{ patient.label }}" variant="tertiary">
  <hx-icon name="pencil"></hx-icon>
</hx-icon-button>
```

### Healthcare Use Cases
- Close/dismiss buttons on alerts and dialogs
- Table row actions (edit, delete, view)
- Toolbar buttons in dashboard interfaces
- Mobile-optimized compact actions

### Acceptance Criteria
1. `label` property is REQUIRED — console warning if missing
2. Renders `aria-label` from label property
3. Tooltip shows label text on hover/focus
4. All variants match hx-button visual language
5. Focus ring visible on keyboard navigation
6. Works with any icon system (slot-based, not icon-name-based)
7. ElementInternals form participation
8. Storybook stories for all variants and sizes
title: T1-02: hx-icon-button — Icon-Only Action Button

<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-icon-button/hx-icon-button.stories.ts`:
- Around line 159-183: The base Storybook render function does not bind the
public props href, name, and value from argTypes, so those controls are
non-functional; update the base render template (the render function used by the
stories in hx-icon-button.stories.ts) to spread and pass the component props
(including href, name, value) from args into the rendered component (or into the
<a> / <button> branches) so the argTypes controls wire up correctly and work in
all stories that use the shared render.
- Around line 236-249: Remove the non-null assertions used in the story
play/render code (e.g., hxIconButton!, hxIconButton!.shadowRoot!, innerButton!,
args.href!) by adding proper type-narrowing checks or optional chaining: check
that hxIconButton and hxIconButton.shadowRoot exist before querying and either
return/throw or fail the test if missing, use const innerButton =
hxIconButton.shadowRoot?.querySelector('button') and guard with if
(!innerButton) { /* handle */ } before calling userEvent.click, and validate
args.href with if (typeof args.href !== 'string') { /* handle */ } — apply the
same pattern across the play functions referenced (including the locations
around hxIconButton, innerButton, shadowRoot and args.href) so no non-null
assertions remain.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-icon-button/hx-icon-button.styles.ts`:
- Around line 17-27: The component styles contain hardcoded fallback literals;
replace all literal values in hx-icon-button.styles.ts (e.g., the border
width/solid, border-radius rem value, background-color hex/transparent, color
hex, transition durations/timing like 150ms ease, and text-decoration) with the
corresponding design tokens

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: The test at line 65-71 calls warnSpy.mockRestore() at the end, but if the expect assertion at line 69 throws an error (i.e., the assertion fails), mockRestore() is never called. This leaves console.warn mocked for all subsequent tests in the suite, causing test pollution and unpredictable failures. This is a real bug that can cause intermittent test failures in CI. The fix is minimal and well-justified.
